stem: A loan application enters the loan processing pipeline. The probability of passing application review is 89%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing credit check is 82%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing underwriting is 86%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing approval is between 77% and 90% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). What is the overall probability that the loan application is approved?

ground_truth.answer: P(approved) ranges from 0.483 to 0.565 as the approval pass rate varies from 77% to 90%. Point estimate: 0.521.

solution_steps: The loan application must pass all 4 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3)×P(stage 4).
The uncertainty in the approval stage creates a 8.2 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the approval rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
